items.headline: Auto Sales Dealership Approved for El Camino Real
items.summary: The Planning Commission approved a special permit to convert 975 W. El Camino Real into an auto sales lot. The existing commercial building will remain unchanged; the project only includes repaving, restriping, and landscape upgrades.

items.headline: Financial assistance pilot program launches for water customers
items.summary: San José is launching a pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers, likely aimed at helping residents manage utility costs. Details on eligibility and program scope were approved by the City Council on May 12.

items.headline: 27 townhomes approved including 5 affordable units
items.summary: The Planning Commission approved a three-story residential project with 27 townhomes, of which 5 units will be affordable housing. This mixed-income development adds housing capacity to Cupertino while meeting affordable housing requirements.

items.headline: Lawsuit filed against city over SummerHill Homes development project
items.summary: A case (Mark Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino) is in litigation involving SummerHill Homes, a real estate party, though the specific claims and allegations remain sealed in closed session discussions with the city attorney.

items.headline: City Council considers 72-unit affordable housing project on parking lot
items.summary: The Council heard a public hearing on a six-story, 100% affordable housing development at 450 Lytton Avenue that would replace a city-owned 52-space parking lot with 72 residential units. The project is seeking density bonus concessions under state law due to its affordable housing status.

items.headline: Electrical infrastructure upgraded for R&D expansion at Porter Drive
items.summary: A permit was issued to install new electrical transformers and conduit along Porter Drive to support increased power demands from new research and development operations at 3210 Porter Drive. The project indicates growing R&D activity in Palo Alto's commercial corridor.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting federal immigration enforcement on city property
items.summary: City Council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil immigration enforcement activities. This shields city facilities from being used in immigration operations and reflects Campbell's stance on local control over immigration enforcement.

items.headline: Council declares overgrown brush a public nuisance
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted a resolution classifying hazardous vegetation and brush as a public nuisance, which enables the city to enforce removal on private properties. This move gives the city legal grounds to address fire-risk overgrowth that residents may have neglected.
